Name: Python End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
description: The Python version on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_result: The "Python" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
detection_method: Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.
Details: Python End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
impact: An EOL version of Python is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unxed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.
solution: Update the Python version on the remote host to a still supported version.

Name: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.17, 3.5.x < 3.5.7, 3.6.x < 3.6.9, 3.7.x < 3.7.3 Information Disclosure Vulnerability (bpo-36216) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Information disclosure (credentials, cookies, etc. that are cached against a given hostname).
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: Python is aected by improper Handling of Unicode Encoding (with an incorrect netloc) during NFKC normalization.

Name: Python < 3.9.5 Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to an authentication bypass vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the target system.
insight: The vulnerability exists in the ipaddress library, which mishandles leading zero characters in the  octets of an IP address string. This would allow an attacker to bypass IP-based access control.

Name: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.17, 3.5.x < 3.5.8, 3.6.x < 3.6.9, 3.7.x < 3.7.4 Information Disclosure Vulnerability (bpo-36742) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Information disclosure (credentials, cookies, etc. that are cached against a given hostname).
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: 'urlsplit' is aected by a NFKD normalization vulnerability in 'user:password@'.
Note: The x for 'CVE-2019-9636' ignored the 'user/password' before '@' whereas it still allowed to exploit the vulnerability.

Name: Python <= 3.10.x Buer Overow Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a buer overow vulnerability in the _sha3 module.

Name: Python < 2.7.14, 3.3.x < 3.3.7, 3.4.x < 3.4.7, 3.5.x < 3.5.4, 3.6.x < 3.6.2 Expat 2.2 (bpo-29591) - Linux
description: Python is prone to various security vulnerabilities in bundled expat.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 2.7.14, versions 3.3.x prior to 3.3.7, 3.4.x prior to 3.4.7, 3.5.x prior to 3.5.3 and 3.6.x prior to 3.6.2.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2016-0718: Expat allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a malformed input document, which triggers a buffer overflow.

- CVE-2016-4472: The overflow protection in Expat is removed by compilers with certain optimization settings, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via crafted XML data.
NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-1283 and CVE-2015-2716.

Name: Python < 2.7.14, 3.4.x < 3.4.8, 3.5.x < 3.5.5 PyString_DecodeEscape integer overow (bpo-30657) - Linux
description: CPython (aka Python) is prone to an integer overow vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 2.7.14, versions 3.4.x prior to 3.4.8 and 3.5.x prior to 3.5.5.
insight: An integer overow in the 'PyString_DecodeEscape' function in 'stringobject.c' is resulting in a heap-based buer overow (and possible arbitrary code execution).

Name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an innite loop vulnerability leading to a denial of service (DoS).
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.6.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2024-6232: Regular expressions that allowed excessive backtracking during tarle.TarFile header parsing are vulnerable to ReDoS via specically-crafted tar archives.

- CVE-2024-7592: When parsing cookies that contained backslashes for quoted characters in the cookie value, the parser would use an algorithm with quadratic complexity, resulting in excess CPU resources being used while parsing the value.

- CVE-2024-8088: When iterating over names of entries in a zip archive (for example, methods of 'ziple.Path' like 'namelist()', 'iterdir()', etc) the process can be put into an innite loop with a maliciously crafted zip archive. This defect applies when reading only metadata or extracting the contents of the zip archive.This vulnerability only aects ziple.Path, while the more common API ziple.ZipFile class is unaected.
- CVE-2024-45490, CVE-2024-45491, CVE-2024-45492: Vulnerabilities in libexpat prior to version 2.6.3

Name: Python < 3.6.13, 3.7.x < 3.7.10, 3.8.x < 3.8.7, 3.9.x < 3.9.1 Python Issue (bpo-41944) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: If an attacker can compromise the pythontest.net server, they gain arbitrary code execution on all buildbots.
If an attacker has control over the network connection of a machine running the Python test suite, they gain arbitrary code execution there.
insight: By default, the tests are not run with network resources enabled and so the Python test suite is safe. But if the Python test suite is run explicitly with the 'network' resource enabled (-u network or -u all command line option), the CJK codecs tests of the Python test suite run eval() on content received via HTTP from pythontest.net.

Name: Python 2.7 Command Injection Vulnerability (Sep 2018) - Linux
description: Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python 2.7 Command Injection Vulnerability (Sep 2018) - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.118272 Version used: 2023-07-05T05:06:18Z
impact: This can result in Denial of service, Information gain via injection of arbitrary les on the system or entire drive. This attack appear to be exploitable via Passage of unltered user input to the function.
This vulnerability appears to have been xed in versions after commit 'add531a1e55b0a739b0f42582f1c9747e5649ace'.
solution: See the referenced vendor advisory for a solution.
insight: Python (CPython) version 2.7 contains a command injection vulnerability in shutil module.

Name: Python < 3.6.13, 3.7.x < 3.7.10, 3.8.x < 3.8.8, 3.9.x < 3.9.2 Python Issue (bpo-42938) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a buer overow vulnerability in 'PyCArg_repr'.  Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.6.13, versions 3.7.x prior to 3.7.10, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.8 and 3.9.x prior to 3.9.2.
insight: A buer overow in 'PyCArg_repr' in '_ctypes/callproc.c' exists, which may lead to remote code execution in certain Python applications that accept oating-point numbers as untrusted input, as demonstrated by a '1e300' argument to 'c_double.from_param'. This occurs because sprintf is used unsafely.

Name: Python < 2.7.14, 3.3.x < 3.3.7, 3.4.x < 3.4.7, 3.5.x < 3.5.4, 3.6.x < 3.6.2 Expat 2.2.1 (bpo-30694) - Linux
description: 'Expat' in Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 2.7.14, versions 3.3.x prior to 3.3.7, 3.4.x prior to 3.4.7, 3.5.x prior to 3.5.4 and 3.6.x prior to 3.6.2.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:

- CVE-2012-0876: The XML parser (xmlparse.c) in expat computes hash values without restrict- ing the ability to trigger hash collisions predictably, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via an XML le with many identiers with the same value.
- CVE-2016-0718: Expat allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a malformed input document, which triggers a buer overow.
- CVE-2016-9063: An integer overow during the parsing of XML using the Expat library. This vulnerability aects Firefox < 50.

- CVE-2017-9233: XML External Entity vulnerability in libexpat 2.2.0 and earlier (Expat XML Parser Library) allows attackers to put the parser in an innite loop using a malformed external entity denition from an external DTD.

Name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 2.7.14, versions 3.3.x prior to 3.3.7, 3.4.x prior to 3.4.7, 3.5.x prior to 3.5.4 and 3.6.x prior to 3.6.2.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:

- CVE-2012-0876: The XML parser (xmlparse.c) in expat computes hash values without restrict- ing the ability to trigger hash collisions predictably, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via an XML le with many identiers with the same value.
- CVE-2016-0718: Expat allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a malformed input document, which triggers a buer overow.
- CVE-2016-9063: An integer overow during the parsing of XML using the Expat library. This vulnerability aects Firefox < 50.

- CVE-2017-9233: XML External Entity vulnerability in libexpat 2.2.0 and earlier (Expat XML Parser Library) allows attackers to put the parser in an innite loop using a malformed external entity denition from an external DTD.
The following aws exist:
- CVE-2024-12718: Allows modifying some le metadata (e.g. last modied) with lter='data' or le permissions (chmod) with lter='tar' of les outside the extraction directory.
- CVE-2025-4138: Allows creating arbitrary symlinks outside the extraction directory

Name: Python 2.x < 2.7.17, 3.5.x < 3.5.8, 3.6.x < 3.6.9, 3.7.x < 3.7.4 Protection Bypass Vulnerability (bpo-35907) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a protection bypass vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python 2.x prior to version 2.7.17, versions 3.5.x prior to 3.5.8, 3.6.x prior to 3.6.9 and 3.7.x prior to 3.7.4.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: urllib supports the 'local_le:' scheme, which makes it easier for remote attackers to bypass protection mechanisms that blacklist 'le:' URIs, as demonstrated by triggering a 'urllib.urlopen('local_le:///etc/passwd')' call.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.13.11 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.
Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12) but specic patch versions are not yet available.
solution: Update to version 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.
insight: When reading an HTTP response from a server, if no read amount is specied, the default behavior will be to use Content-Length. This allows a malicious server to cause the client to read large amounts of data into memory, potentially causing out-of-memory (OOM) or other DoS conditions.

Name: Python Command Injection Vulnerability (Oct 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a command injection vulnerability in the venv module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: A vulnerability has been found in the venv module and CLI where path names provided when creating a virtual environment were not quoted properly, allowing the virtual environment creator to inject commands into virtual environment 'activation' scripts (ie 'source venv/bin/activate'). This means that attacker-controlled virtual environments are able to run commands when the virtual environment is activated. Virtual environments which are not created by an attacker or which aren't activated before being used (ie './venv/bin/python') are not aected.
insight: A vulnerability has been found in the venv module and CLI where path names provided when creating a virtual environment were not quoted properly, allowing the virtual environment creator to inject commands into virtual environment 'activation' scripts (ie 'source venv/bin/activate'). This means that attacker-controlled virtual environments are able to run commands when the virtual environment is activated. Virtual environments which are not created by an attacker or which aren't activated before being used (ie './venv/bin/python') are not aected.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: There is a defect in the CPython 'tarle' module aecting the 'TarFile' extraction and entry enumeration APIs. The tar implementation would process tar archives with negative osets without error, resulting in an innite loop and deadlock during the parsing of maliciously crafted tar archives.

Name: Python Shell Command Injection Vulnerability (bpo-24778) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a shell command injection vulnerability in the mailcap module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.7.16, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.16, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.16 and 3.10.x prior to 3.10.8.
insight: In Python (aka CPython) the mailcap module does not add escape characters into commands discovered in the system mailcap le. This may allow attackers to inject shell commands into applications that call mailcap.ndmatch with untrusted input (if they lack validation of user- provided lenames or arguments).

Name: Python < 3.6.11, 3.7.x < 3.7.7, 3.8.x < 3.8.2 Use After Free Vulnerability (bpo-39421) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an use after free vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.6.11, versions 3.7.x prior to 3.7.7, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.2 and 3.9.x prior to 3.9.0.
insight: A use-after-free exists in Python via heappushpop in heapq.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Oct 2022) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.7.16, versions 3.8.x prior to 3.8.16, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.16, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.9 and version 3.11.0.
insight: An unnecessary quadratic algorithm exists in one path when processing some inputs to the IDNA (RFC 3490) decoder, such that a crafted, unreasonably long name being presented to the decoder could lead to a CPU denial of service. Hostnames are often supplied by remote servers that could be controlled by a malicious actor. In such a scenario, they could trigger excessive CPU consumption on the client attempting to make use of an attacker-supplied supposed hostname. For example, the attack payload could be placed in the Location header of an HTTP response with status code 302.

Name: Python < 2.7.17, 3.x < 3.5.8, 3.6.x < 3.6.10, 3.7.x < 3.7.5 Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (bpo-34155) - Linux
description: The email module in Python is vulnerable due to improper input validation.

Name: Python < 3.7.17, 3.8.x < 3.8.17, 3.9.x < 3.9.17, 3.10.x < 3.10.12, 3.11.x < 3.11.4 RCE Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python versions prior to 3.7.17, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.17, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.17, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.12 and 3.11.x prior to 3.11.4.
insight: An issue in the urllib.parse component allows attackers to bypass blocklisting methods by supplying a URL that starts with blank characters.

Name: Python <= 3.12.1 'RecursionError' Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a 'RecursionError' vulnerability.
detection_result: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
NOTE: email.utils.parseaddr is categorized as a Legacy API in the documentation of the Python email package. Applications should instead use the email.parser.BytesParser or email.parser.Parser class.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
product_detection_result: Product: cpe:/a:python:python:2.7.13   Method: Python Detection Consolidation   OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.112857)
impact: The legacy email.utils.parseaddr function in Python through 3.12.1.
insight: The legacy email.utils.parseaddr function allows attackers to trigger 'RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object' via a crafted argument. This argument is plausibly an untrusted value from an application's input data that was supposed to contain a name and an e-mail address.
NOTE: The vendor's perspective is that this is neither a vulnerability nor a bug. The email package is intended to have size limits and to throw an exception when limits are exceeded.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Mar 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in libexpat.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.8.19, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.19, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.14, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.9 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.3.
insight: libexpat allows a denial of service (resource consumption) because many full reparsings are required in the case of a large token for which multiple buffer fills are needed.

Name: Python urllib.parse Vulnerability (bpo-43882) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a vulnerability urllib.parse.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.6.14, version 3.7.x through 3.7.10, 3.8.x through 3.8.10 and 3.9.x through 3.9.4.
insight: A aw was found in Python, specically within the urllib.parse module. This module helps break Uniform Resource Locator (URL) strings into components. The issue involves how the urlparse method does not sanitize input and allows characters like '\r' and '\n' in the URL path. This aw allows an attacker to input a crafted URL, leading to injection attacks.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (bpo-44022) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.6.14, version 3.7.x through 3.7.10, 3.8.x through 3.8.10 and 3.9.x through 3.9.5.
insight: If a client request a HTTP/HTTPS/FTP service which is controlled by attacker, attacker can make this client hang forever, even if the client has set a timeout argument.

Name: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.16, 3.4.x < 3.4.10, 3.5.x < 3.5.7, 3.6.x < 3.6.7, 3.7.0 Python Issue (bpo-34623) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation allows denial of service attacks against Expat by constructing an XML document that would cause pathological hash collisions in Expat's internal data structures, consuming large amounts CPU and RAM.
insight: The aw exists because Python's elementtree C accelerator fails to initialise Expat's hash salt during initialization

Name: Python < 2.7.15, 3.x < 3.4.9, 3.5.x < 3.5.6, 3.6.x < 3.6.5, 3.7.x < 3.7.0.beta3 Python Issue (Issue32981) - Linux
description: Python is prone to multiple denial of service (DoS) vulnerabilities.
impact: Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to conduct a denial of service attack on the aected user.
insight: Python is failing to sanitize against backtracking in:
- CVE-2018-1060: pop3lib's apop method
- CVE-2018-1061: 'diib.IS_LINE_JUNK' method

Name: Python <= 3.7.2 DoS Vulnerability (bpo-36260) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
insight: 'Lib/ziple.py' in Python allows remote attackers to cause a DOS (resource consumption) via a ZIP bomb.

Name: Python <= 3.8.3 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to deny legitimate users access to the application or exhaust a system's resources.
solution: Updates are available. Please see the references for more information.
insight: An attacker is able to craft a TAR archive leading to an infinite loop when opened by tarle.open, because _proc_pax lacks header validation.

Name: Python < 3.10.6 Information Disclosure Vulnerability (bpo-43223) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python < 3.10.6 Information Disclosure Vulnerability (bpo-43223) - Linux
impact: Python prior to version 3.10.6.
insight: An open redirection vulnerability in the HTTP server when an URI path starts with '//'.This may lead to information disclosure.

Name: Python < 3.5.10, 3.6.x < 3.6.12, 3.7.x < 3.7.9, 3.8.x < 3.8.5 Python Issue (bpo-39603) - Linux
description: http.client in Python is prone to CRLF injection.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: If the attacker controls the HTTP request method, the http.client in Python allows CRLF injection.
insight: It is possible to inject HTTP headers via the HTTP method which doesn't reject newline characters.

Name: Python 2.7.x <= 2.7.17, 3.5 <= 3.5.9, 3.6.x <= 3.6.10, 3.7.x <= 3.7.6, 3.8.x <= 3.8.1 Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to crash the application.
insight: The AbstractBasicAuthHandler class of the urllib.request module uses an inecient regular expression (catastrophic backtracking) which can be exploited by an attacker to cause a denial of service.

Name: Python < 3.7.0 DoS Vulnerability (Mar 2018) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This vulnerability allows attackers to cause a DoS (divide-by-zero and exception) via a crafted wav format audio le.
Note: CVE is disputed by vendor, although xes have been applied.
Aected Software/OS Python through version 3.6.4.
solution: Update to Python version 3.7.0 or later.
insight: The Wave_read._read_fmt_chunk function in Lib/wave.py does not ensure a nonzero channel value.

Name: Python < 2.7.17, 3.5.x < 3.5.8, 3.6.x < 3.6.10, 3.7.x < 3.7.5 XSS Vulnerability (bpo-38243)
description: Python is prone to a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 2.7.17, versions 3.x prior to 3.5.8, 3.6.x prior to 3.6.10 and 3.7.x prior to 3.7.5.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: The documentation XML-RPC server has XSS via the 'server_title' field. This occurs in 'Lib/DocXMLRPCServer.py' in Python 2.x, and in 'Lib/xmlrpc/server.py' in Python 3.x. If 'set_server_title' is called with untrusted input, arbitrary JavaScript can be delivered to clients that visit the http URL for this server.

Name: Python 2.x < 2.7.18, 3.x < 3.5.10, 3.6.x < 3.6.11, 3.7.x < 3.7.8, 3.8.x < 3.8.3 CRLF Injection Vulnerability (bpo-38576) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a CRLF injection vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python 2.x prior to version 2.7.18, versions 3.x prior to 3.5.10, 3.6.x prior to 3.6.11, 3.7.x prior to 3.7.8 and 3.8.x prior to 3.8.3.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: http.client allows to pass control characters like CRLF newlines which can be abused to inject HTTP headers.

Name: Python 2.x < 2.7.17, 3.x < 3.5.8, 3.6.x < 3.6.9, 3.7.x < 3.7.4 HTTP Header Injection Vulnerability (bpo-30458) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a HTTP header injection vulnerability (follow-up of CVE-2016-5699).
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python versions 2.x prior to 2.7.17, 3.x prior to 3.5.8, 3.6.x prior to 3.6.9 and 3.7.x prior to 3.7.4.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:

- CVE-2019-9740: An issue was discovered in urllib2 in Python 2.x and urllib in Python 3.x. CRLF injection is possible if the attacker controls a url parameter, as demonstrated by the rst argument to 'urllib.request.urlopen' with 'rn' (specically in the query string after a ' ?' character) followed by an HTTP header or a Redis command.

- CVE-2019-9947: An issue was discovered in urllib2 in Python 2.x and urllib in Python 3.x. CRLF injection is possible if the attacker controls a url parameter, as demonstrated by the rst argument to 'urllib.request.urlopen' with 'rn' (specically in the path component of a URL that lacks a ' ?' character) followed by an HTTP header or a Redis command.

Name: Python < 3.6.13, 3.7.x < 3.7.10, 3.8.x < 3.8.8, 3.9.x < 3.9.2 Web Cache Poisoning Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a web cache poisoning vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation can result in malicious requests being cached as completely safe ones, as the proxy would usually not see the semicolon as a separator, and therefore would not include it in a cache key of an unkeyed parameter.
insight: The package python is vulnerable to Web Cache Poisoning via urllib.parse.parse_qsl and urllib.parse.parse_qs by using a vector called parameter cloaking. When the attacker can separate query parameters using a semicolon, they can cause a dierence in the interpretation of the request between the proxy (running with default conguration) and the server.

Name: Python < 3.6.13, 3.7.x < 3.7.10, 3.8.x < 3.8.7, 3.9.x < 3.9.1 Race Condition Vulnerability (bpo-40791) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a race condition vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.6.13, versions 3.7.x prior to 3.7.10, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.7 and 3.9.x prior to 3.9.1.
insight: An issue was discovered in compare_digest in Lib/hmac.py. Constant-time-defeating optimisations were possible in the accumulator variable in hmac.compare_digest.

Name: Python < 3.6.14, 3.7.x < 3.7.11, 3.8.x < 3.8.9, 3.9.x < 3.9.3 Python Issue (bpo-42988) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability via pydoc getle.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: A local or adjacent attacker who discovers or is able to convince another local or adjacent user to start a pydoc server could access the server and use it to disclose sensitive information belonging to the other user that they would not normally be able to access.
solution: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.6.14, versions 3.7.x prior to 3.7.11, 3.8.x prior to 3.8.9 and 3.9.x prior to 3.9.3.
insight: Running 'pydoc -p' allows other local users to extract arbitrary les.
The '/getle?key=path' URL allows to read arbitrary le on the lesystem.

Name: Python Symlink Dereference Vulnerability (Mar 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a symlink dereference vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Symlink Dereference Vulnerability (Mar 2024) - Linux
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.8.19, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.19, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.14, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.8 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.1.

The temple.TemporaryDirectory class would dereference symlinks during cleanup of permissions-related errors. This means users which can run privileged programs are potentially able to modify permissions of les referenced by symlinks in some circumstances.
insight: The temple.TemporaryDirectory class would dereference symlinks during cleanup of permissions-related errors. This means users which can run privileged programs are potentially able to modify permissions of les referenced by symlinks in some circumstances.

Name: Python < 2.7.17, 3.x < 3.4.10, 3.5.x < 3.5.7, 3.6.x < 3.6.9, 3.7.x < 3.7.3 Cookie domain check returns incorrect results (bpo-35121) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an improper input validation vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: When a program uses http.cookiejar.DefaultPolicy and tries to do an HTTP connection to an attacker-controlled server, existing cookies can be leaked to the attacker.
insight: http.cookiejar.DefaultPolicy.domain_return_ok in Lib/http/cookiejar.py does not correctly validate the domain: it can be tricked into sending existing cookies to the wrong server.
An attacker may abuse this aw by using a server with a hostname that has another valid hostname as a sux (e.g., pythonicexample.com to steal cookies for example.com).

Name: Python < 3.6.14, 3.7.x < 3.7.11, 3.8.x < 3.8.9, 3.9.x < 3.9.3 (bpo-43285) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This aw allows an attacker to set up a malicious FTP server, that can trick FTP clients into connecting back to a given IP address and port.

Name: Python <= 2.7.18, 3.x <= 3.12.3 Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a security bypass vulnerability in the e-mail module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python <= 2.7.18, 3.x <= 3.12.3 Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
impact: The e-mail module incorrectly parses e-mail addresses which contain a special character. This vulnerability allows attackers to send messages from e-mail addresses that would otherwise be rejected.
solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
Note: The vendor mentions, that a patch was included in main branch 3.13. This version is expected to release in October 2024. The patch might be included in the release of version 3.13 or later.
insight: Python versions through 2.7.18 and 3.x through 3.12.3.

Name: Python < 3.8.18, 3.9.x < 3.9.18, 3.10.x < 3.10.13, 3.11.x < 3.11.5 Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
description: Python is prone to a security bypass vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Instances of ssl.SSLSocket are vulnerable to a bypass of the TLS handshake and included protections (like certificate verification) and treating sent unencrypted data as if it were post-handshake TLS encrypted data if the socket is closed before initiating its own handshake.
insight: Python version prior to 3.8.18, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.18, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.13 and 3.11.x prior to 3.11.5.

Name: Python Email Header Injection Vulnerability (Aug 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an email header injection vulnerability in the email module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Email Header Injection Vulnerability (Aug 2024) - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.114731 Version used: 2024-10-11T15:39:44Z
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.5.
insight: The email module didn't properly quote newlines for email headers when serializing an email message allowing for header injection when an email is serialized.

Name: Python SSL Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a vulnerability in the ssl module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python SSL Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.152433 Version used: 2024-10-11T15:39:44Z
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.14, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.9 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.3.
insight: A defect was discovered in the Python 'ssl' module where there is a memory race condition with the ssl.SSLContext methods 'cert_store_stats()' and 'get_ca_certs()'. The race condition can be triggered if the methods are called at the same time as certicates are loaded into the SSLContext, such as during the TLS handshake with a certicate directory congured.

Name: Python 'ziple' Module Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a vulnerability in the 'ziple' module.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python prior to version 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12, 3.13.x prior to 3.13.10 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.
insight: The 'ziple' module would not check the validity of the ZIP64 End of Central Directory (EOCD) Locator record oset value would not be used to locate the ZIP64 EOCD record, instead the ZIP64 EOCD record would be assumed to be the previous record in the ZIP archive. This could be abused to create ZIP archives that are handled dierently by the 'ziple' module compared to other ZIP implementations. Remediation maintains this behavior, but checks that the oset specied in the ZIP64 EOCD Locator record matches the expected value.

Name: Python Buer Over-Read Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a buer over-read vulnerability in SSLContext.set_npn_protocols().
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Buffer Over-Read Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Linux
impact: Python versions prior to 3.10.
solution: Suggested mitigation by the vendor is one of the following:
- Update to Python 3.10 or later where NPN isn't supported
- Avoid providing an empty list as a parameter to SSLContext.set_npn_protocols()
insight: The product doesn't disallow conguring an empty list ('[]') for SSLContext.set_npn_protocols() which is an invalid value for the underlying OpenSSL API. This results in a buer over-read when NPN is used (see CVE-2024-5535 for OpenSSL). This vulnerability is of low severity due to NPN being not widely used and specifying an empty list likely being uncommon in-practice (typically a protocol name would be congured).

Name: Python IP Ranges Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a vulnerability in the ipaddress module.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python IP Ranges Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Linux
impact: Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.4.
insight: The 'ipaddress' module contained incorrect information about whether certain IPv4 and IPv6 addresses were designated as 'globally reachable' or 'private'. This aected the 'is_private' and 'is_global' properties of the ipaddress.IPv4Address, ipaddress.IPv4Network, ipaddress.IPv6Address, and ipaddress.IPv6Network classes, where values wouldn't be returned in accordance with the latest information from the IANA Special-Purpose Address Registries.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Update to Python 3.9.25, 3.15.0 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x, 3.13.x, 3.14.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.
Aected Software/OS Python versions prior to 3.9.25 and 3.10.x prior to 3.15.0.
Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14) but specic patch versions are not yet available.
insight: If the value passed to os.path.expandvars() is user-controlled a performance degradation is possible when expanding environment variables. This vulnerability exhibits quadratic complexity behavior that could be exploited for denial of service attacks.

Name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Mar 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2024-0450: Quoted zip-bomb protection for ziple
- No CVE: crash in socket.if_indextoname() with a specic value (UINT_MAX).
- No CVE: .pth les with names starting with a dot or containing the hidden le attribute are not skipped

Name: Python 2.7 < 3.4 DoS Vulnerability (bpo-14621) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python 2.7 before 3.4.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: Python only uses the last eight bits of the prex to randomize hash values, which causes it to compute hash values without restricting the ability to trigger hash collisions predictably and makes it easier for context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via crafted input to an application that maintains a hash table.

Name: Python < 3.2.6, 3.3.x < 3.3.3 Wildcard DoS Vulnerability (bpo-17980) - Linux
description: Python's ssl.match_hostname() trips over crafted wildcard names.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Python before 3.2.6 and 3.3 before 3.3.3.
solution: The vendor has released updates. Please see the references for more information.
insight: If the name in the certicate contains many * characters (wildcard), matching the compiled regular expression against the host name can take a very long time.

Name: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an use after free vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Linux
impact: Python prior to version 3.9.23, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.18, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.13, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.11 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.4.
insight: There is an issue in CPython when using bytes.decode(). If you are not using the 'unicode_escape' encoding or an error handler your usage is not aected. To work-around this issue you may stop using the error= handler and instead wrap the bytes.decode() call in a try-except catching the DecodeError.

Name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Jun 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS Python prior to version 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.6.
insight: The html.parser.HTMLParser class had worse-case quadratic complexity when processing certain crafted malformed inputs potentially leading to amplied denial of service.

Name: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.15 Heap-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability Python Issue (bpo-31530)
description: Python is prone to a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability.

Name: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.15 Heap-Based Buer Overow Vulnerability Python Issue (bpo-31530)
description: Python is prone to a heap-based buer overow vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python 2.7.x < 2.7.15 Heap-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability Python Issue (bp.
impact: Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to cause a heap-based buer overow.
insight: The aw exists due to an improper notication mechanism on buer reallocation and corruption
in a le's internal readahead buer which, while processing large amounts of data with multiple
threads, could create a condition where a buer that gets allocated with one thread is reallocated
due to a large size of input.

Name: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an improper input validation vulnerability in the urllib.parse.urlsplit and
urlparse standard functions.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Linux
insight: The Python standard library functions urllib.parse.urlsplit and urlparse accepted domain names
that included square brackets which isn't valid according to RFC 3986. Square brackets are only
meant to be used as delimiters for specifying IPv6 and IPvFuture hosts in URLs. This could
result in dierential parsing across the Python URL parser and other specication-compliant
URL parsers.

Name: Python SSRF Vulnerability (Nov 2024) - Linux
description: Python is prone to a server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Python versions prior to 3.9.21, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.16 and 3.11.x prior to 3.11.4.
insight: The urllib.parse.urlsplit() and urlparse() functions improperly validated bracketed hosts ([]), allowing hosts that weren't IPv6 or IPvFuture. This behavior was not conformant to RFC 3986 and potentially enabled SSRF if a URL is processed by more than one URL parser.

Name: Python Improper Encoding of Output Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux
description: Python is prone to an improper encoding of output vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Python Improper Encoding of Output Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux

OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.171266   Version used: 2025-10-10T05:39:02Z   Product Detection Result   Product: cpe:/a:python:python:2.7.13   Method: Python Detection Consolidation   OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.112857)
impact: Aected Software/OS Python versions prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.4.
insight: During an address list folding when a separating comma ends up on a folded line and that line is to be unicode-encoded then the separator itself is also unicode-encoded. Expected behavior is that the separating comma remains a plan comma. This can result in the address header being misinterpreted by some mail servers.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
detection_result: A web server is running on this port
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

Name: Allowed HTTP Methods Enumeration
description: Enumerates which HTTP methods are allowed.
detection_result: The following list contains the URLs and corresponding supported HTTP methods.
insight: - Basic HTTP methods: GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, CONNECT, OPTIONS, TRACE

- Extended HTTP methods: ACL, BASELINE-CONTROL, BIND, CHECKIN, CHECK- OUT, COPY, LABEL, LINK, LOCK, MERGE, MKACTIVITY, MKCALENDAR, MKCOL, MKREDIRECTREF, MKWORKSPACE, MOVE, ORDERPATCH, PATCH, PRI, PROPFIND, PROPPATCH, REBIND, REPORT, SEARCH, UNBIND, UNCHECKOUT, UNLINK, UN- LOCK, UPDATE, UPDATEREDIRECTREF, VERSION-CONTROL
log_method: Sends multiple HTTP requests and checks the responses.
- This enumeration script is provided 'as is' (means no support is given) and only providing rudimentary information about the possible enabled methods
- This script doesn't guarantee completeness or full reliability
For more reliable determination of the enabled HTTP methods please inspect the conguration of the web server manually and directly on the target host.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 70%
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.40.1):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.

Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection. If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.

Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

Name: Python Detection Consolidation
description: Consolidation of Python detections.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
